name: Endive with Tomato Rosettes
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate the leaves from the heads of Belgian endive, using 6 leaves per serving. Save any remaining leaves and centers for salad later.
2. Rinse the leaves under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
3. In a small bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth.
4. Gradually add milk while continuing to beat until well blended.
5. Stir in the chopped dried tomatoes.
6. Fill a decorating bag with a large rosette nozzle. Pipe the filling onto each basil leaf or watercress sprig on the wide end of each Belgian endive leaf.
7. Arrange the leaves on a platter and garnish with flowers.
8. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Makes 3 dozen hors d'oeuvres
